2605.10831.title: SLIM: Sparse Latent Steering for Interpretable and Property-Directed LLM-Based Molecular Editing
2605.10831.paper_summary: Large language models possess strong chemical reasoning capabilities, making them effective molecular editors. However, property-relevant information is implicitly entangled across their dense hidden states, providing no explicit handle for property control: a substantial fraction of edits fail to improve or even degrade target properties. To address these issues, we propose SLIM (Sparse Latent Interpretable Molecular editing), a plug-and-play framework that decomposes the editor's hidden states into sparse, property-aligned features via a Sparse Autoencoder with learnable importance gates. Steering in this sparse feature space precisely activates property-relevant dimensions, improving editing success rate without modifying model parameters. The same sparse basis further supports interpretable analysis of editing behavior. Experiments on the MolEditRL benchmark across four model architectures and eight molecular properties show consistent gains over baselines, with improvements of up to 42.4 points.
2605.10831.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型擁有強大的化學推理能力，使其成為有效的分子編輯器。<lb/>然而，與性質相關的信息在其密集的隱藏狀態中隱含交織，未提供性質控制的明確手段：相當一部分編輯未能改善甚至降低目標性質。<lb/>為了解決這些問題，我們提出了SLIM（稀疏潛在可解釋分子編輯），這是一個即插即用的框架，通過具有可學習重要性閘的稀疏自編碼器將編輯器的隱藏狀態分解為稀疏的、與性質對齊的特徵。<lb/>在這個稀疏特徵空間中的引導精確地激活與性質相關的維度，提高了編輯成功率，而不修改模型參數。<lb/>相同的稀疏基礎進一步支持對編輯行為的可解釋分析。<lb/>在四種模型架構和八種分子性質的MolEditRL基準上的實驗顯示，與基線相比一致性地獲得增益，改善幅度高達42.4分。
2605.10831.author: Mingxu Zhang et.al.
2605.10831.authors: Mingxu Zhang, Yuhan Li, Lujundong Li, Dazhong Shen, Hui Xiong, Ying Sun
